channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China holds large military exercises surrounding Taiwan to warn against independence
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China deployed an aircraft carrier, other ships and warplanes in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating sealing off key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan Strait.
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Washington state's landmark climate law hangs in the balance in November
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A groundbreaking law that forces companies in Washington state to reduce their carbon emissions while raising billions of dollars for climate programs could be repealed by voters this fall, less than two years after it took effect.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man with loaded gun arrested at checkpoint near Donald Trump's weekend rally in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Nevada man with a shotgun, loaded handgun, ammunition and several fake passports in his vehicle was arrested at a security checkpoint outside Donald Trump's rally Saturday night in the Southern California desert, authorities said. He was released the same day on $5,000 bail.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Shohei Ohtani stolen base streak ends at 36 in NLCS, caught for first time since July 22
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers keep on winning in the playoffs, even without a bunch of home runs and stolen bases by Shohei Ohtani.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers tie postseason mark of 33 straight scoreless innings, top Mets 9-0 in NLCS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — After spending the first seven years of his big league career back East, Jack Flaherty came home. He joined a winning team in the Los Angeles Dodgers and helped make a bit of playoff history.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republican lawsuits target rules for overseas voters, but those ballots are already sent
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The latest method of voting to fall into the political crosshairs is the way overseas voters — including members of the military stationed abroad — cast their ballots. The process is governed by federal law and implemented by states.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Maybe advanced civilizations aren’t all that advanced: Scientist
item.description: Antonio Gelis-Filho writes that perhaps advanced civilizations have reached the limits of their technology, and we may be close to doing the same.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls for adding 10,000 Border Patrol agents after derailing a bipartisan border bill
item.description: PRESCOTT VALLEY, Ariz. (AP) — Former President Donald Trump on Sunday proposed hiring 10,000 additional Border Patrol agents and giving them a $10,000 retention and signing bonus, after he derailed a bipartisan bill earlier this year that included funding for more border personnel.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Indigenous Peoples' Day: Here's what's open and closed in San Diego on Monday
item.description: Indigenous Peoples' Day, also known as Columbus Day, is celebrated on the second Monday in October, with many stores remaining open but with no mail and some banks closed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 50 Cent-themed escape room coming to Shreveport, Louisiana
item.description: The event is free, but you must sign up for a time slot during the library's "Fall into the West" festival or by calling (318) 635-0883. If you are age 16 and under, you must have an adult accompany you into the escape room.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Tripp, NewsNation

item.title: Sam's Club opening location in Texas, but there's no checkout lanes
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the warehouse club chain will soon open a store in Texas but there won't be any checkout lanes.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 200 miles of Blue Ridge Parkway reopens as long-term work remains in North Carolina
item.description: The Blue Ridge Parkway was closed indefinitely after Hurricane Helene swept through the region last month.
item.dc:creator: QCN Digital Staff, Fox 5

item.title: Cops nab man allegedly carrying illegal guns near Trump's Coachella rally; suspect says he backs ex-president
item.description: Authorities arrested a man outside former President Donald Trump's Coachella Valley campaign rally on Saturday in possession of a shotgun, a loaded handgun, and a high-capacity magazine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deputies 'probably' prevented 3rd Trump assassination attempt, California sheriff says
item.description: A man was arrested outside of a campaign rally for former President Donald Trump in Southern California, and multiple guns were illegally in his possession, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Video shows mechanical SpaceX arms catching Starship rocket booster back at the launch pad
item.description: SpaceX pulled off the boldest test flight yet of its enormous Starship rocket on Sunday, catching the returning booster back at the launch pad with mechanical arms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prison operator under federal scrutiny spent millions settling Tennessee mistreatment claims
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The leading private prison company in the U.S. has spent more than $4.4 million to settle dozens of complaints alleging mistreatment — including at least 22 inmate deaths — at its Tennessee prisons and jails since 2016.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, TRAVIS LOLLER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In an engineering feat, mechanical SpaceX arms catch Starship rocket booster back at the launch pad
item.description: SpaceX pulled off the boldest test flight yet of its enormous Starship rocket on Sunday, catching the returning booster back at the launch pad with mechanical arms.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What you should do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives — emails, social media and bank accounts, photos, chat messages and more — that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Family desperately trying to get Colorado dad home after he fell ill on European cruise
item.description: A Colorado family is desperately trying to get their dad from Englewood home after a sudden and severe illness in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Kerridge, WGN 9

item.title: What is the purpose of an open-front toilet seat?
item.description: Open-front toilet seats — or “U-shaped” seats — are common in public bathrooms or commercial spaces, but they’re almost never the norm in private residences.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Clyburn says he's 'concerned' about Black men voting for Trump
item.description: Rep. James Clyburn (D-S.C.) said he is “concerned” that Black men will vote for former President Trump in the upcoming election — or sit it out entirely. “I am concerned about Black men staying home or voting for Trump,” Clyburn said Sunday on CNN’s “State of the Union.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Mayorkas: Hurricane misinformation 'is extremely pernicious'
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas on Sunday described the flurry of misinformation online about the hurricanes devastating parts of the U.S. as "extremely pernicious," warning that it’s hindering efforts to assist those affected by the storms.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Euthanasia, pronouns, sports betting: Intriguing 2024 ballot measures
item.description: Several states have placed important measures on their ballots, ranging from flag designs to end-of-life care.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina football player dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: UNC had honored Tylee Craft, 23, during Saturday's game. Coach Mack Brown, however, said he hadn't learned of Craft's death until after the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tested the limits on using the military at home. If elected again, he plans to go further
item.description: During his first term as president, Donald Trump tested the limits of how he could use the military to achieve policy goals.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From the pulpit, Harris calls out Trump for hurricane misinformation. Biden surveys Florida's damage
item.description: Kamala Harris has used an appearance before a largely Black church audience in battleground North Carolina to call out Donald Trump for spreading misinformation about the federal government's hurricane response.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Arizona man found dead in Starbucks bathroom after allegedly shooting himself
item.description: A man shot and killed himself in a Starbucks bathroom in Gilbert, Arizona, after he acted erratically and pointed a gun at another person, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iowa mom's boyfriend nearly got away with murder — until a zipper changed everything
item.description: Dr. Michael Baden recounts how a nondescript zipper imprint helped prosecutors put Ivan "Sam" Brammer behind bars in the death of his girlfriend, Ilene Gowan, in a new Fox Nation show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Floridians cleaning up from Hurricane Milton are hampered by a widespread fuel shortage
item.description: CORTEZ, Fla. (AP) — Floridians recovering from Hurricane Milton, many of whom were journeying home after fleeing hundreds of miles to escape the storm, spent much of Saturday searching for gas as a fuel shortage gripped the state. In St.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY ONEIL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ lawsuit filed against Virginia for voter list purge
item.description: With Election Day just around the corner, the Commonwealth is in hot water after the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced it is suing the state for illegally striking voters off of voter lists.
item.dc:creator: Allison Williams, NewsNation

item.title: Puerto Rico 'most wanted' homicide fugitive arrested in North Carolina
item.description: A "highly dangerous" fugitive accused of murder in Puerto Rico has been arrested in Halifax County, according to multiple agencies.
item.dc:creator: Starletta Watson, NewsNation

item.title: Catholic League accuses Whitmer of mocking Eucharist with Dorito
item.description: Podcaster Liz Plank posted a video on her Instagram showing herself being fed a Dorito by Whitmer, who's standing above Plank.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Rising chocolate prices impact Halloween candy choices
item.description: Despite rising chocolate and candy prices, Americans are expected to spend $3.5 million on Halloween sweets this year.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Allred pushes back on Cruz transgender attack ads
item.description: “Ted Cruz is lying again, but now he's lying about our children. I'm a dad. I'm also a Christian,” Allred said on camera.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4
